mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling on a sustained basis, discount rates and recession odds both decline, so S&P 500 prices should rise over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if rate cuts are already fully anticipated or if equity weakness is driven by a separate growth shock that dominates policy easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in a majority of periods when FEDFUNDS is trending down, or if the rolling 3-year rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A jump in UNRATE above its recent baseline signals labor-market deterioration, which raises recession risk and compresses expected corporate earnings. That combination should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days once the unemployment move is large enough to be meaningful.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal loses significance after the unemployment rise threshold is met.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening payroll income and retail spending, which should improve near-term revenue expectations and support S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should be strongest when both labor income and consumption are improving together rather than separately.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-RSAFS improvement does not outperform either proxy alone, or if the combined signal has a rolling 3-year hit rate below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO while CPIAUCSL is stable or easing indicates improving real output without renewed inflation pressure, a favorable mix for equity margins and valuation. That setup should predict positive S&P 500 returns over roughly one trading month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% m/m while CPIAUCSL rises <= 0.2% m/m
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not produce higher average 20-day SPX returns than unconditional returns, or if inflation-stable industrial upswings fail to outperform industrial upswings with rising CPI.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS level tightens financial conditions through discount-rate and credit channels, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days when policy remains restrictive. The mechanism fails when high rates are accompanied by strong growth and falling inflation that offset the valuation drag.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate periods are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the 3-year rolling IC is persistently below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth slows while RSAFS also weakens, aggregate demand is losing momentum from both production and consumption sides, which should depress expected earnings and forward equity returns. The signal is most relevant over a one-quarter horizon because the growth slowdown needs time to appear in prices.
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS are not associated with negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal adds no incremental explanatory power over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in PAYEMS during a post-shock recovery signals rehiring and income repair, which should lift risk appetite and support S&P 500 returns over the next week to month. The effect should fade once employment gains stop accelerating or if payroll growth is offset by a simultaneous rise in unemployment.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 100k m/m and UNRATE is flat or falling
failure_condition: Reject if payroll gains with stable or falling unemployment do not produce positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal disappears in rolling 2-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls while INDPRO is also weakening, it points to a broad cyclical slowdown in real activity and construction demand, which should pressure earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The pattern should be strongest in a late-cycle environment rather than during a temporary weather-related dip.
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in housing starts and industrial production do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal is indistinguishable from random over any 3-year rolling window.
